mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS series signals easier discount rates and a lower hurdle rate for equity valuation, so S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days are positive when the policy rate is declining from an already low level.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward S&P 500 returns are not positive more than 55% of the time during months when FEDFUNDS is falling, or if rolling 3-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE above its recent trend indicates deteriorating labor income and consumption demand, which should pressure equities lower over the next month as earnings expectations are revised down.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not show negative 20-day forward returns at least 55% of the time, or if the sign flips positive in a majority of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in RSAFS implies stronger retail demand and firmer near-term revenue growth, so equity prices should rise over the next 20 trading days when retail sales are improving on a monthly basis.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS increases month-over-month for 2 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if two-month retail-sales improvement is not followed by positive 20-day S&P 500 returns more than 55% of the time, or if the signal's 3-year rolling IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When INDPRO rises while CPIAUCSL is flat or declining, real activity is expanding without immediate inflation pressure, supporting margins and valuation multiples; this combination predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the joint condition does not outperform either series alone, or if triggered months do not produce positive 20-day returns in at least 55% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in PAYEMS and rise in UNRATE identifies a labor-market contraction that weakens consumption and earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls month-over-month while UNRATE rises month-over-month
failure_condition: Reject if the joint labor deterioration signal fails to deliver negative 20-day returns in at least 55% of occurrences, or if its Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A high FEDFUNDS rate above 4.5 percent tightens financial conditions and increases the equity discount rate, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next 5 trading days during restrictive policy periods.
failure_condition: Reject if restrictive-policy episodes do not produce negative 5-day returns more than 55% of the time, or if the sign is positive during repeated high-rate windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rebound in HOUST after prior weakness signals housing-demand stabilization and improving cyclical sentiment, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as investors price a broader recovery.
candidate_trigger: HOUST rises for 3 consecutive months after a 6-month decline
failure_condition: Reject if housing-rebound episodes fail to generate positive 60-day returns more than 55% of the time, or if the 60-day IC is near zero across rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a recessionary repair phase, rising INDPRO together with falling UNRATE identifies a broad cyclical inflection in production and employment, which should produce strong positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the combined production-employment inflection does not outperform baseline 60-day returns, or if positive returns are not observed in at least 60% of matched episodes.
